## S4.01 Testing Student Reg {#s4.01-testing-student-reg}

**Owner:** Directors · **Last reviewed:** 2025 board (imported from the Hitchhiker's Guide, July 2026) · **Status:** current — when you find something stale, fix it and update this line.

Before you test, the following things need to be done:

* [Line items](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/accounting/lineitemtype/) need to be added for your program for lunch and shirts.  
  * For Sprout, you will need 3 line items for lunch: "Lunch week 1", "Lunch week 2", "Lunch week 3". Splash just needs one.  
  * Lunch should look something like this, with as many options as choices you have for lunch:  
  * ![][image6]  
  * Shirts should look similar, but the amount dec should be 10.00, and the options should be S (adult), M (adult), L (adult), XL (adult).  
    * If you don’t specify adult sizes, people might assume kids’ sizes and the sizes will be icky.  
* **DO NOT DO THE FOLLOWING STEP WHILE AN EMAIL IS SENDING ON WEBSITE.**  
  To check if an email is sending, go to the back end and “email texts”. If the top item doesn’t have a send time in the right most column (if it says “none” or is blank), you cannot create a custom form (the website will shut down).  
* You need to create the custom form for your program and link it to student registration. This asks about what they're excited about and their allergies. It's important especially to see if a school teacher mass registered all their students, because in that case the responses would all be the same. Editing custom forms is hard.  
  * Go to this [link](https://yale.learningu.org/customforms/), and click on "create" near the top.  
  * Go down to "Create from an existing form" and choose the most recent form relevant to Sprout/Splash.  
  * Change the name field to match your program semester/year.  
  * Go to the links tab and change it so that it links to your program.  
  * Submit the form and hope for the best.  
  * Next, click on "Fill" next to your program's form at the same link.   
  * Look at the URL of the form that appeared. It should be something like: "[https://yale.learningu.org/customforms/view/97/](https://yale.learningu.org/customforms/view/97/)"  
  * Memorize that number at the end of the URL. In this case, 97\.  
  * Go [here](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/customforms/form/), and click on your form. Memorize the link ID \#.  
  * Next, go [here](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/tagdict/tag/). Create a new tag and make it look like this, but put that number you memorized in the value field and the link ID \# in the Object id field. Title it "learn\_extraform\_id" just like here, and have the content type be "program", just like shown:  
    ![][image7]  
  * Keep hoping for the best, but you should be good now.

Okay. So you have maybe completed the necessary prerequisites to student registration. 

* Check your deadlines and make sure students will have access at the correct time and it will end at the correct time.  
* Make sure the separate deadlines for registration mainpage & confirm reg are open until the end of your program date, and that credit card payment, and financial aid are open for a day or two beyond the end of student registration.

Find a fake student's account whose grade is in the right range. For example, "ktest".

* Give this account permissions.  
  * Go [here](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/users/permission/)  
  * Add a new permission, giving the user all student permissions for the program for a certain amount of time. **Do NOT fill out the role field.** If you put student there, anyone with the role of student will have those permissions. That is NOT what you want (this would be equivalent to changing something on the deadlines page).  
* Search for the user on the main website and morph into them. Go to your student reg link, and check that you can fill out the form, make lunch and shirt purchases, and register for classes.  
  * Weird things that might come up:  
    * Is lunch a block that you can sign up for?  
    * Do all the blocks exist…  
* Once you are done, make sure to go and remove this fake student from your program. To do this, go [here](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/program/studentregistration/) and search for the user. Select all the checkboxes relevant to your program, and press "expire student registrations". Also go [here](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/accounting/transfer/), search for the user, select the checkboxes relevant to your program, and press "delete selected transfers."

[image6]: <data:image/png;base64,>
[image7]: <data:image/png;base64,>
